title: As a reviewer, I want Credit Score AI guidelines that distinguish valid credit-score inquiries from vague credit talk, so that scoring is consistent
description:
Feature: Credit Score AI guidelines for Suco - Home Improvement

Scenario: Agent asks about credit score or rating explicitly
Given a transcript where the agent asks for the customer's credit score, rating, or qualitative credit quality
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be Yes

Scenario: Agent mentions credit vaguely without asking about score or quality
Given a transcript where the agent references credit but does not ask about score, rating, or qualitative quality
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be No

Scenario: Agent never asks about credit score or rating
Given a transcript with no credit score inquiry
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be No

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Pulled the current Credit Score AI score from Plexus. Existing guidelines are missing, so I am drafting an initial binary-classifier guidelines document from the live score YAML and will validate it before any push.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validated the new Credit Score AI guidelines with skills/plexus-guidelines/validate_guidelines.py and pushed them to Plexus as score version 34c751a9-adc4-416e-b174-3027e89df178. The new version is not champion-promoted; this change adds the missing guidelines document.
